title: calytrix ecalycata subsp. ecalycata
intro: Calytrix ecalycata subsp. ecalycata is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae, commonly known as a type of citrus bush, native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Calytrix ecalycata subsp. ecalycata include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits and interactions with other medications.
